expectations.text: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
expectations.evidence: coverage_matrix.md contains 6 strategy tables (Requirement-Based, Equivalence Class, Boundary Value, Error Handling, Internal State, Configuration Variants) each with columns Design Element, Test Case(s), and Strategy. All 46 tests are mapped to their corresponding design elements.

expectations.text: Every behavior rule (startup clamping, cruise rate-of-change, emergency shutdown, was_clamped flag) has at least one test
expectations.evidence: Startup clamping: TestStartupModeBehavior has 3 tests (clamps_to_minimum, clamps_to_maximum, passes_within_bounds). Cruise rate-of-change: TestCruiseModeBehavior has 3 rate-of-change tests (increasing, decreasing, within_limit). Emergency shutdown: TestEmergencyShutdownBehavior has 2 tests (forces_zero_rate, already_zero_still_clamped). was_clamped flag: TestWasClampedFlag has 2 tests (true_when_rate_differs, false_when_rate_unchanged).

expectations.text: Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
expectations.evidence: TestErrorHandling class contains: test_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero (line 339), test_negative_requested_rate_in_cruise (line 347), test_negative_elapsed_time_skips_rate_of_change_limit (line 354), test_unrecognized_mode_treated_as_emergency_shutdown (line 364), test_empty_string_mode_treated_as_emergency_shutdown (line 371). All three error categories have dedicated tests.

expectations.text: Boundary values tested for requested_rate (0.0, negative, at STARTUP_MIN, at STARTUP_MAX, at CRUISE_MAX)
expectations.evidence: 0.0: test_at_cruise_min_zero (line 262) and test_cruise_allows_zero_rate (line 93). Negative: test_negative_requested_rate_treated_as_zero (line 339) with -10.0. At STARTUP_MIN (10.0): test_at_startup_min (line 240). At STARTUP_MAX (50.0): test_at_startup_max (line 247). At CRUISE_MAX (200.0): test_at_cruise_max (line 267). Additional boundary tests include just_below_startup_min (9.99) and just_above_startup_max (50.01).

expectations.text: No test uses 'assert does not raise' or 'assert no exception' as its only assertion
expectations.evidence: All 46 tests use concrete value assertions (assert result.actual_rate == X, assert result.was_clamped is True/False, assert result.clamping_reason == 'string'). No test contains 'does not raise', 'no exception', or pytest.raises used as a sole assertion. Every test verifies specific output values.

expectations.text: Test names describe the scenario being tested, not just the method name
expectations.evidence: All test names are scenario-descriptive: test_startup_clamps_rate_to_minimum_when_below, test_cruise_applies_rate_of_change_limit_increasing, test_negative_elapsed_time_skips_rate_of_change_limit, test_previous_rate_updates_after_each_call, test_custom_max_rate_change, etc. No test is named after the method (e.g., no test_limit or test_limit_method).

expectations.text: Stateful behavior tested: rate-of-change limiting requires multiple sequential calls
expectations.evidence: TestStatefulBehavior class (lines 383-419) has 4 tests with sequential calls. test_previous_rate_updates_after_each_call makes 3 sequential calls verifying state accumulates correctly (50->100->150). Rate-of-change tests in TestCruiseModeBehavior (lines 99-127) also require two sequential calls to establish previous_rate before testing the limit.

expectations.text: Configuration variants tested: at least one test with non-default config values
expectations.evidence: TestConfigurationVariants class (lines 427-457) has 4 tests using custom_limiter fixture with non-default values: startup_min_rate=20.0 (default 10.0), startup_max_rate=80.0 (default 50.0), cruise_max_rate=300.0 (default 200.0), max_rate_change=50.0 (default 100.0). Each config parameter is individually exercised.
